category_title: At-risk four-year-old students exhibiting varied oral language behaviors
minimal_context: The student is playing in the block center with peers and communicating about their building activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for center play. What are you doing in the block center?

STUDENT: (To peer) I am going to build a castle in the block center. (To tutor) Castle.

TUTOR: What is going to go inside the castle?

STUDENT: Dogs.

TUTOR: Tell me more about that.

STUDENT: Big dogs. (To peer) If you don’t give me that toy, you’ll be sorry!

options.A: Record the behavioral data in scatter plots and ABC charts when center time finishes.
options.B: Collaboratively identify social-emotional targeted goals with clearly defined definitions of desirable and undesirable behaviors.
options.C: Utilize one-on-one experiences and dramatic play experiences to support the student during center time.
options.D: Adopt a 'leaky' and 'viscous' orientation to control, knowing unruliness will recur, rather than instituting an inflexible disciplinary regime.
